chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR. C.P.No. D – 274 of 2023. 1.For orders on o/obj. at flag.A. 2.For Hearing of CMA 1261/2023. 3.For Hearing of main case. - Petitioner: Khadim Hussain and another, through Sohail Ahmed Khoso Advocate. Respondents: Province of Sindh and others through Shaharyar Awan Asstt.A.G. Date of Hearing: 29.11.2023. ORDER YOUSUF ALI SAYEED, J- The Petitioners claim to be aggrieved by an Office order dated 13.01.2023 issued by the Respondent No.7, being the Registrar of Trade Unions, reflecting that the Respondent No.8, who is a private person, has been elected as the General Secretary of the SCARP Irrigation Employees Union, Division Naushahro Feroze, by its General Body. Per the Petitioners, the Respondent No.8 is ineligible as he is a retired employee and is even otherwise unsuitable as he is complicit in corruption. As such, it has been sought that the Office order be declared null and void and that directions be issued to the Respondent No.6, being Chairman of the Anti- Corruption Establishment, to conduct an enquiry. Be that as it may, it is apparent that the Petitioners have not challenged the candidature of the Respondent No.8 at the relevant time, nor made any proper representation to the Registrar in that regard. Indeed, on query posed, learned counsel simply drew our attention to a letter dated 12.01.2023 addressed by the Petitioners to the Registrar, with a perusal thereof revealing it to be silent on the subject of eligibility. The Petition is found to be misconceived and stands dismissed accordingly, along with the pending miscellaneous applications. JUDGE JUDGE Akber.
